reasoning: The yfinance tool gives a combined estimate of 0.61 for the stock resolving higher than its extremely depressed current price of 68.19 (the 1-year low). The stock has sold off heavily in anticipation of Q2 earnings on August 5, likely driven in part by macro news (tariffs) and weak technical momentum. Pre-earnings selloffs frequently lead to 'buy the news' mean reversion if results are not disastrous, favoring an initial bounce. However, following the instruction to stay close to 0.5 for weekly stock moves and discounting the tool's 0.61 estimate due to the high variance introduced by upcoming earnings, I set the initial probability at 0.56. As the horizon expands, the effect of any immediate mean reversion diminishes, so probabilities decay towards 0.5 to reflect increasing fundamental uncertainty over time, as explicitly requested.
